expectations.text: Identifies commercial-investigation intent and maps it to a comparison/listicle format
expectations.evidence: The brief explicitly identifies 'Commercial investigation' intent and specifies 'Listicle/comparison article' as the matching content type.

expectations.text: Title under 60 chars and meta description 150-160 chars
expectations.evidence: The brief notes a title option at 47 characters and labels the meta description as 156 characters, within the 150-160 range.

expectations.text: Includes an H2/H3 outline and People-Also-Ask questions
expectations.evidence: The outline includes multiple H2 sections with H3 sub-entries per CRM, and a dedicated 'People Also Ask / Questions to Answer' section with six questions.

expectations.text: Includes a differentiation angle, E-E-A-T notes, internal links, and a CTA
expectations.evidence: The brief has dedicated sections for Differentiation Angle (section 8), E-E-A-T Signals (section 11), Internal Links (section 9), and Primary CTA (section 10).

expectations.text: Flags that real search volume/difficulty need a tool rather than inventing numbers
expectations.evidence: The brief states 'Pull actual volume and difficulty data from Ahrefs or Semrush before finalizing' and qualifies its estimate as approximate.
